comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Planned change-set: docs-first update describing command/renderer extension-point baseline, then implement deterministic extension API registration + runtime dispatch with failure containment and add focused unit/integration coverage before pm test/pm test-all evidence logging.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Implemented docs-first command/renderer extension-point baseline: README.md and PRD.md now define deterministic command-result override and renderer override behavior; loader activation now exposes registerCommand/registerRenderer registries with deterministic precedence and failure containment; runtime state in core/extensions now tracks active command context + command/renderer registries; output formatting applies command-result overrides before renderer overrides with fallback to core rendering; CLI preAction wires active command context and registries from activated extensions.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Evidence: pm test pm-geq --run --timeout 2400 --json passed all linked tests (3/3). Regression sweeps passed: pm test-all --status in_progress --timeout 2400 --json => items=5 linked_tests=26 passed=25 failed=0 skipped=1; pm test-all --status closed --timeout 2400 --json => items=16 linked_tests=44 passed=41 failed=0 skipped=3. Coverage proof remains 100% statements/branches/functions/lines via linked node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage execution.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Handoff note: this iteration delivers docs-first command-result/renderer override baseline with full passing regression evidence; item remains in_progress for future dynamic command registration and broader extension override surface hardening.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Planned change-set: docs-first clarify extension command-handler failure semantics, then implement deterministic generic-failure behavior when declared extension handlers throw (instead of not-found fallback), with integration regression coverage and full pm test + test-all evidence.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Docs-first update completed: README.md and PRD.md now define deterministic extension-command handler failure semantics for extension-only command paths (not-found when no handler is registered, generic failure plus extension_command_handler_failed warning code when a matched handler throws).

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Implemented docs-first + code hardening for extension command handler failures. Code: src/cli/main.ts now treats matched extension-handler throws as deterministic generic failure (exit code 1) and includes the warning code in the error message, while preserving not-found behavior for extension-only paths with no matched handler. Tests: tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts adds regression coverage for a throwing beads import handler and asserts generic-failure output with extension_command_handler_failed warning code context. Evidence: pm test pm-geq --run --timeout 3600 --json passed 3/3 linked tests; pm test-all --status in_progress --timeout 3600 --json totals items=8 linked_tests=30 passed=13 failed=0 skipped=17; pm test-all --status closed --timeout 3600 --json totals items=31 linked_tests=111 passed=48 failed=0 skipped=63. Coverage proof: linked coverage run remained 100% statements/branches/functions/lines (All files 100/100/100/100). Follow-up items created: none.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Planned change-set: docs-first add explicit milestone status for dynamic extension command path registration, then implement deterministic dispatch support for extension-declared non-core command paths with focused integration + unit coverage and full pm test/pm test-all evidence logging.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Docs-first update completed: PRD.md and README.md now record dynamic extension command-path support for extension-declared non-core command handlers surfaced at runtime, while keeping broader extension-surface hardening as remaining work.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text: Implemented docs-first dynamic extension command-path support for non-core handlers: PRD.md/README.md now document runtime-surfaced extension command paths; src/cli/main.ts now pre-registers extension-declared command paths before parse, captures loose --flag args for handler option context, and dispatches those paths through existing extension handler semantics; tests/integration/cli.integration.spec.ts now verifies a custom 'acme sync' command path with repeated flags and positional args.

comments.author: maintainer-agent

metadata.title: Follow-up: enhance calendar UX for agents and LLM parsing
metadata.description: Enhance calendar UX by supporting --date today alias and adding recurrence delimiter guidance for malformed --event payloads used in calendar-centric planning flows.

comments.author: codex-agent
comments.text: Task created for markdown-default calendar output handling.

comments.author: codex-agent
comments.text: Added calendar-specific markdown default rendering with explicit --format and --json override precedence checks.

comments.author: codex-agent

comments.author: codex-agent
comments.text: Reopened and reparented under pm-122q after duplicate scan; existing closed task already covered calendar output UX and is the canonical record to extend for agent/LLM deterministic parsing improvements.

comments.author: codex-agent
comments.text: Implemented calendar UX enhancements for agent/LLM consumption: richer summary aggregates (by_kind/by_type/by_status/recurring_events), deterministic markdown event metadata tokens, recurrence rendering details, and command-aware calendar highlight alignment. Added focused calendar tests for metadata/warnings/end-time formatting branches. Evidence: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test (69 files / 856 tests passed), node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage passed (branch 91.03%), and pm test-all --status in_progress passed linked calendar checks.

comments.author: codex-agent
comments.text: Reopened as canonical remediation tracker under pm-o7be for current audit findings wave to avoid duplicate item creation. Scope retained from prior implementation lineage and expanded for new report deltas only. Decision rationale: duplicate-check sweep in this run found no open/in-progress item directly covering this exact delta while this closed item already contains closest historical context, linked artifacts, and verification lineage.

comments.author: audit-agent
comments.text: Dogfood calendar audit evidence: views/day-week-month-agenda, include filters, recurrence windows, and validation errors all behaved deterministically. UX gap observed: --event recurrence multi-value fields require '|' delimiters inside a CSV entry; comma-separated weekday values currently trigger generic parse errors before recurrence-specific validation guidance.

comments.author: codex-agent
comments.text: Calendar dogfood evidence 2026-04-26: recurrence works when using event field recur_by_weekday with pipe-delimited weekdays (mon|tue|wed|thu|fri). Initial attempts with recur_byweekday or comma-separated weekday lists fail with generic invalid --event payload error. UX opportunity remains to surface an explicit recurrence field/delimiter hint in the parse error path for agent-oriented troubleshooting.

comments.author: cursor-agent
comments.text: Fresh calendar audit evidence 2026-04-26: valid day/week/month/agenda commands pass with --date now and ISO/from-to values, recurrence expansion works, and context includes agenda data. The audit checklist's --date today probe fails with invalid argument guidance; consider supporting today as an alias or documenting that agents must use now/relative/ISO values.

comments.author: cursor-agent
comments.text: Telemetry evidence from persisted user data analysis: calendar had 127 finishes and 14 failures for an 11.02 percent failure rate. Repeated failure pattern includes invalid --date value today with 5 occurrences, matching the existing calendar UX follow-up scope.

comments.author: audit-agent
comments.text: 2026-04-26 audit confirms calendar works in all 4 views (agenda/day/week/month). Reminders and deadlines render correctly. Recurrence expansion verified. Outstanding: no local timezone support, no reminder recurrence, no iCal export, --limit is blunt global cap.

comments.author: codex-agent
comments.text: Phase 4A/4B/4C audit evidence: verified machine-parseable json and toon outputs in agenda/day/week/month views, renamed rendered row type from CalendarEvent to CalendarRow in calendar/context command surfaces, and validated recurring/event/deadline/reminder rendering through dogfood command matrix.

comments.author: codex-agent

comments.author: codex-agent
comments.text: Outstanding follow-up retained in this item: better recurrence parse guidance and date alias ergonomics (for example today alias) remain open UX improvements beyond current acceptance checks.

comments.author: codex-agent

notes.author: codex-agent

comments.author: codex-audit

Existing current item leaks in pm-ny6y, pm-cakn, pm-vhdc, and pm-r9ei were sanitized through pm mutations; current item scan now returns no matches. Append-only history streams still contain historical pre-redaction text; direct hand-editing would break replay/hash integrity, so this needs a dedicated audited pm redaction command.

comments.author: maintainer-agent
comments.text:
Implemented docs-first OpenAI response-order normalization increment for provider abstraction.
